notes: Paper content complete. PDF compilation failed due to LaTeX graphics package incompatibility with local TeX installation. Full LaTeX source available at exam_paper/6_paper/paper.tex

1_load_and_profile.notes: Successfully profiled 3 datasets: HCW mandates (16 states), population data (52 states), COVID-19 metadata

2_research_questions.notes: Formulated primary ecological study question examining association between HCW mandates and COVID-19 mortality

3_acquire_data.notes: Downloaded COVID-19 surveillance data from NY Times GitHub (61,942 rows filtered to 6,805 for study period)

4_statistical_analysis.notes: 3 regression models fitted. Key finding: mandates associated with 23.4 fewer deaths per 100k (95% CI: -11.5 to -35.2, p<0.001)

5_generate_figures.notes: 2 figures and 2 tables generated in JAMA style

6_literature_review.notes: 12 references compiled from peer-reviewed literature and data source documentation

7_write_paper.notes: Complete JAMA-style paper with structured abstract (7 sections), Key Points, Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, Limitations, Conclusions, Supplement

8_compile_and_review.notes: LaTeX source complete but PDF compilation failed due to graphics package incompatibility with local TeX installation. Paper content is valid and complete.

key_findings.exposure: State-level HCW COVID-19 vaccine mandates (16 of 52 jurisdictions)
key_findings.primary_result: Mandates associated with 23.4 fewer deaths per 100,000 (95% CI: -11.5 to -35.2; p < 0.001)
key_findings.interpretation: 47% relative reduction in mortality after adjustment for region and population
